title: aspidium guadalupense
intro: Aspidium guadalupense, also known as the Guadalupe fern or Maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Aspidium guadalupense include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against infection. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and ointments, which can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in hot water or oil.
